concerns.text: FR-007 mandates a sensitivity analysis on 'decision threshold for interaction term significance' by sweeping cutoffs {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}. The Idea and Constitution (Principle VII) define success via model performance (RMSE/R²) and a paired t-test on errors, not via a specific 'significance threshold' for interaction terms. The Idea mentions identifying 'which interaction terms... capture non-linear mixing effects' but does not specify a statistical or SHAP-magnitude threshold to sweep. This requirement appears to be an unrequested feature (over-reach) that redefines the evaluation metric from 'predictive accuracy' to 'threshold stability' without upstream justification.

SC-004 measures success by 'confirming that headline rates (false-positive/negative) do not vary drastically' across the threshold sweep. The Idea's 'Expected results' section defines falsification as 'performance comparable to or worse than the baseline' (RMSE/R²), not by the stability of false-positive rates derived from an arbitrary threshold sweep. This success criterion introduces a new, unrequested research question regarding threshold robustness that is not present in the upstream idea.

The Assumptions section states: 'if the idea implies a specific community-standard threshold (e.g., |SHAP| > 0.1), that value is used as the baseline for the sweep.' The upstream Idea does not imply any specific community-standard threshold for SHAP values or interaction term significance. This assumption attempts to justify the unrequested FR-007/SC-004 by inventing a premise not found in the source material.
